column1: # Residual orthogonality null. Residuals r_l(t)=c_l(t)-MeanEmbedding(t). Observed pairwise angles (rad) over n=210 timestamps with 3 valid layers
column3: seed 42): Null1 = Gaussian random 384-d vectors rescaled to each timestamp's observed residual norms; Null2 = layer labels permuted across timestamps (vectors kept
column4: reassigned within layer). Question: is the observed distribution TIGHTER or DISPLACED vs the random-vector null
column5: NOT whether angles are near pi/2 (pi/2=1.5708).

column1: # CONCLUSION: observed angles deviate from the null in: DISPLACED vs Null1_gaussian_rescaled (mean +0.524 rad); DISPLACED vs Null2_layer_label_perm (mean +0.521 rad) (KS p<0.05 for all-pairs vs both nulls: True).
# MECHANISM: MeanEmbedding(t) is EXACTLY the equal-weight mean of the three layer centroids (||MeanEmbedding - (c_S+c_M+c_R)/3|| = 0 to machine precision)
column2: so r_S+r_M+r_R = 0 by construction. Three equal-norm vectors summing to zero have pairwise angle arccos(-1/2)=120deg=2.094 rad; observed all-pairs mean is 2.094 rad. The near-orthogonality framing is therefore an algebraic artifact of the residual definition
